title: Musk's Colossus 1 AI supercomputer's inefficient mixed-architecture design couldn't be used to train Grok, so Anthropic's using it for inference instead Musk readies unified Blackwell-only Colossus 2 for frontier training and potential IPO
summary: Anthropic leases Colossus 1 from SpaceX to alleviate Claude's compute bottlenecks, potentially expanding AI infrastructure ambitions.
insight: Musk repurposes underutilized AI supercomputer for inference, freeing resources for unified training.

title: Australian who smuggled 50 pounds of cocaine hidden inside printers get nine years behind bars five devices intercepted by border forces had compressed powder stuffed in the paper trays
summary: An Australian smuggler received a nine-year sentence for cocaine hidden in printers. Five printers were intercepted by border forces containing compressed powder. The smuggler was part of a crime syndicate.
insight: Creative smuggling methods can lead to severe consequences

title: AMD GPU owners take to Reddit to report fan problem with driver update Zero RPM feature could cause GPU temperatures to rise unexpectedly
summary: AMD GPU owners report Zero RPM feature malfunction with driver update, causing GPU temperatures to rise unexpectedly when monitor goes to sleep or turns off.
insight: GPU temperature control compromised by driver update glitch.

title: Microsoft is working on a fix to downgraded GPU drivers in Windows Update new system uses multiple IDs
summary: Microsoft confirms Windows Update downgrades GPU drivers under certain conditions. A partial fix is in development to minimize driver downgrades. The fix is expected to release later this year.
insight: Preventing driver downgrades avoids potential system crashes and performance issues.

title: AMD makes FSR 4 upscaling official for Radeon RX 7000- and 6000-series cards RDNA 3 and RDNA 2 chips will soon enjoy improved visuals
summary: AMD is officially bringing FSR 4 to Radeon RX 7000- and 6000-series cards, making it available to a wider range of users. This feature, previously exclusive to RX 9000-series cards, will bring improved visuals to older Radeons. The update is expected to roll out in the coming months.
insight: Wider compatibility with FSR 4 boosts Radeon performance and visuals.

title: Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II review: Impressively versatile
summary: The Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II headset offers versatile connectivity with multiple 2.4GHz wireless connections using USB-A dongles, making it a highly convenient option for users who need to switch between devices.
insight: Wireless connectivity freedom across multiple devices at once matters.

title: Intel announces McLaren F1 partnership, will battle AMD-powered Mercedes deal includes chips for aerodynamic analysis, vehicle-dynamics simulation, race strategy analytics, and more
summary: Intel partners with McLaren Racing, expanding their presence in F1. The deal involves collaboration on various aspects, including aerodynamic analysis and vehicle-dynamics simulation. This partnership marks a significant development in the tech-racing landscape.
insight: Tech giants invest in F1 to improve performance and innovation

title: Alienware's budget gaming laptop starts at $1,299 Last-gen parts and a plastic shell bring the premium brand into the mainstream
summary: Alienware enters the mainstream with a budget gaming laptop starting at $1,299, using last-gen components in a plastic shell.
insight: Budget-friendly Alienware laptops appeal to a broader gaming audience.

title: Fully-functional RTX 3070 16GB gets frankensteined into existence by harvesting dead PCBs and RX 6800 XT's VRAM chips doubles frame rate in games like Spider Man 2 at 4K and includes switch for 8GB mode
summary: A tech enthusiast has created a functional RTX 3070 with 16GB of VRAM by combining a defective Nvidia card with VRAM chips from an AMD Radeon RX 6800 XT and using a switch to enable 8GB mode.
insight: Reviving dead hardware components can unlock new performance possibilities.

title: Leading-edge foundry roadmaps for TSMC, Intel and Samsung outlining the path to 1.4nm nodes and beyond
summary: Leading-edge foundries TSMC, Intel, and Samsung have divergent roadmaps: TSMC prioritizes predictability, Intel focuses on architectural shifts, and Samsung optimizes yields. Their paths to 1.4nm nodes and beyond differ significantly. Each foundry must balance technological advancements with cost and yield considerations.
insight: Foundry roadmaps diverge, impacting industry's future and innovation pace

title: 70% of Americans oppose data centers near their homes, now less popular than nuclear power plants opposition towards nearby AI infrastructure heating up as tech companies ramp up projects to acquire more compute
summary: A new survey shows 70% of Americans oppose data centers near their homes. This opposition has increased significantly from 47% in a previous survey. Data center projects are becoming more unpopular, especially AI infrastructure.
insight: Rising opposition to AI data centers erodes tech industry's public image.

title: PENOX WristBuds A Bluetooth 6.0 smartwatch with integrated TWS earbuds (Crowdfunding)
summary: The PENOX WristBuds is a rugged Bluetooth 6.0 smartwatch with integrated TWS earbuds, offering a 2-in-1 device that eliminates the need for a separate charging case.
insight: Redefining wearable tech with a clutter-free, all-in-one design experience.

title: M5Stack PaperColor ESP32-S3 devkit features 4-inch E Ink Spectra 6 color display
summary: The M5Stack PaperColor ESP32-S3 devkit features a 4-inch full-color E Ink display, offering low power consumption and high visibility under strong lights. It's equipped with various sensors, buttons, LEDs, and a speaker, and includes a rechargeable 1250 mAh battery. The device supports WiFi, Bluetooth, and microSD storage.
insight: Advancements in ePaper technology for IoT and wearable applications.

title: Start9 RISC-V Router features SpacemiT K1 SoC, runs StartWRT OpenWrt fork (Crowdfunding)
summary: The Start9 RISC-V Router features a SpacemiT K1 SoC with 8-core RISC-V processor, 4GB RAM, and 16GB eMMC flash, offering dual GbE networking and WiFi 6 capabilities. It runs the StartWRT OpenWrt fork, making it highly customizable. The router is available for crowdfunding.
insight: Highly customizable, open-source router for tech enthusiasts and developers.

title: Yocto Project 6.0 Wrynose released with Linux 6.18 LTS
summary: The Yocto Project 6.0, codenamed Wrynose, has been released with Linux 6.18 LTS, offering over 4 years of support and improved security features. This release includes toolchain updates and new tools to ease compliance with regulations. It is the first LTS release since Yocto 5.0.
insight: Enhanced security and compliance features for embedded Linux systems.

title: Best of Sensors: MethaneTrack brings realtime gas leak detection
summary: NevadaNano's MethaneTrack Automated Continuous Emissions Monitoring System won a Best of Sensors award for its innovative real-time gas leak detection capabilities.
insight: Real-time gas leak detection enhances safety and environmental accountability.

title: With Artemis safely home, NASA eyes radiation for future flights
summary: NASA plans to use radiation sensors from Artemis II for future missions, pending full testing on tissue chips. These sensors will help protect astronauts from cosmic radiation. The data collected will aid in developing better shielding for future flights.
insight: Astronaut radiation protection is a top priority for long-term space travel

title: Forced to vibe code at work, programmers say their skills are deteriorating
summary: Programmers are forced to use AI coding, leading to skill decline and frustration.
insight: Forced AI adoption erodes developer skills and creates technical debt issues.

title: Microsoft is baking even more Copilot functionality into the Edge browser
summary: Microsoft is adding more Copilot functionality to the Edge browser, enhancing seamless browsing. This shift focuses on Edge as the primary platform for AI-powered features. Copilot integration in Windows 11 is being scaled back.
insight: Enhancing web browsing with AI-powered features in Edge

title: The Sony Xperia 1 VIII is the anti-flagship flagship: headphone jack, microSD, and thick bezels in 2026
summary: The Xperia 1 VIII boasts a 6.5-inch display with 120Hz refresh, AMOLED technology, and Corning Gorilla Glass Victus 2 protection. It features a headphone jack and microSD card slot. The device has thick top and bottom bezels.
insight: Sony defies modern design trends with nostalgic features in 2026 flagship

title: A security researcher says Microsoft secretly built a backdoor into BitLocker, releases an exploit to prove it
summary: Microsoft's BitLocker allegedly contains a backdoor, dubbed YellowKey, which allows unauthorized access. A researcher has released an exploit to demonstrate the flaw. The bug can be triggered by copying a specific folder to a USB drive.
insight: Potential compromise of secure encryption undermines user trust and security

title: Motorola Razr Fold takes on Samsung and Google with a strong first-gen debut
summary: Motorola's first book-style foldable, the Razr Fold, earns strong early reviews for its design and features.
insight: Motorola challenges Samsung and Google with a competitive foldable debut.

title: 70% of Americans don't want AI data centers near their home, that's more opposition than nuclear plants get
summary: 70% of Americans oppose AI data centers near their homes, a strong opposition. This is more resistance than nuclear plants typically face. The negative media stories about AI data centers contribute to this widespread disapproval.
insight: Negative media coverage fuels public opposition to AI data centers.

title: The Steam Controller's hidden Wilhelm scream Easter Egg is pure Valve genius
summary: The second-gen Steam Controller sold out quickly due to scalpers. A new reservation system aims to address this issue. The controller was initially priced at $99.
insight: Scalpers drive up demand, making high-end products unaffordable for genuine customers.

title: FSR 4 upscaling is finally coming to older Radeon GPUs, and the image quality gains are real
summary: AMD's Radeon RX 7000 and RX 6000 GPUs will support FSR 4.1, with RX 6000 getting support in 2027. Older GPUs will rely on INT8 processing, a slower alternative. This will bring image quality gains to older graphics cards.
insight: FSR 4.1 brings image quality gains to older Radeon GPUs

title: What was Nintendo's first ever product (back in 1889)?
summary: Nintendo's first product was a handmade hanafuda playing card set, produced in 1889. The cards were a type of Japanese playing card used for games and were popular among locals. This early product laid the foundation for Nintendo's future success.
insight: Nintendo's humble beginnings in traditional Japanese card games

title: Forza Horizon 6 GPU Benchmark: 8GB vs. 16GB VRAM
summary: Forza Horizon 6's demanding visuals result in significant VRAM consumption, affecting performance. Testing across various settings and resolutions reveals notable differences between 8GB and 16GB GPUs. The right VRAM choice impacts frame rates and overall gaming experience.
insight: 8GB VRAM may not be enough for Forza Horizon 6's high graphics settings.

title: GoPro explores sale after falling from $11 billion giant to $187 million company
summary: GoPro considers sale or merger amidst shareholder optimism, despite financial struggles.
insight: Strategic options signal potential for drastic change in company direction.

title: Microsoft is finally fixing Windows Update's habit of downgrading your GPU drivers without asking
summary: Microsoft is changing its policy to prevent WU from downgrading GPU drivers.
insight: Prevents unnecessary driver downgrades saving users time and performance.

title: Anthropic says more than 90% of its code is now written by AI
summary: Anthropic's CFO, Krishna Rao, revealed that over 90% of their code is now written by AI. This statement was made on an episode of Patrick O'Shaughnessy's Invest Like the Best podcast. This high percentage highlights the growing reliance on AI in software development.
insight: AI-generated code is rapidly replacing human-written code in tech companies.

title: Two brothers deleted 96 federal databases after being fired one googled how to hide the evidence afterward
summary: Two brothers, Sohaib and Muneeb Akhter, were convicted of conspiracy to commit computer fraud after deleting 96 federal databases. They had previously worked for federal contractor Opexus before being fired. The incident marks their second federal cybercrime conviction.
insight: Former employees pose significant insider threat to sensitive information
